mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware without the need for GPUs. It supports various AI tasks such as text generation, audio, and image processing, making it a versatile choice for local AI deployments. Key trade-offs include the need for local resources and potential limitations in scalability compared to cloud solutions.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing LocalAI deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai-linux-amd64

sudo mv localai-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/localai

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step sets up a local Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is required to run LocalAI.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30.0 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI using Helm.

steps.title: Verify LocalAI Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running correctly in the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This step upgrades LocalAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Drain all LocalAI pods.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the LocalAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Docker image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the repository is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure your Kubernetes cluster is running and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for LocalAI.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources allocated to your Minikube cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved performance for existing models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
